questions.question: What is a singly linked list?
options: A) A collection of elements stored in contiguous memory locations
B) A collection of elements where each element points to the next element
C) A collection of elements where each element points to the previous element
D) A collection of elements stored in a separate memory location
questions.explanation: A singly linked list is a linear data structure where each element is a separate object, and each element points to the next element in the sequence.

questions.question: What is the purpose of representing a linked list as a chain?
options: A) To improve search efficiency
B) To reduce memory usage
C) To improve insertion and deletion efficiency
D) To improve storage efficiency
questions.explanation: Representing a linked list as a chain is useful when we need to traverse the list in a specific order, making it an efficient way to implement operations like insertion and deletion.

questions.question: What is a linked stack?
options: A) A data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle
B) A data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle
C) A data structure that does not have a specific order
D) A data structure that is a combination of stack and queue
questions.explanation: A linked stack is a data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, where elements are added and removed from the top of the stack.

questions.question: What is a linked queue?
options: A) A data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle
B) A data structure that follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle
C) A data structure that does not have a specific order
D) A data structure that is a combination of stack and queue
questions.explanation: A linked queue is a data structure that follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, where elements are added to the rear and removed from the front of the queue.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of a singly linked list?
options: O(n)
O(log n)
questions.explanation: The time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of a singly linked list is O(n), as we need to traverse the list to update all pointers.
